summary: Agent1 considered multiple alloy types' coefficient of friction data from various studies without finding definitive values; however, based on general knowledge that Elgiloy is typically rougher compared to other options such as stainless steel, titanium, and nickel-titanium, they concluded that Elgiloy would have one of the highest coefficients of friction among the listed materials due to its inherent texture facilitating better mechanical interlocking during fabrication procedures despite lacking precise measurements supporting their conclusion directly. To clarify any doubts regarding material choice specifically related to orthodontics applications might require additional specialized testing focusing solely on each metal’s tribological
